Name: Restart-WinProcess.ps1
Description: Stops a named process. If the -Start switch is supplied the process is also restarted after stopping. Process name must be provided as an input.
Impact Notes: Stopping the wrong process can crash applications with unsaved data or destabilise the user session. No validation or safelist is applied before stopping.
Additional Notes: Consider adding a safelist of processes that should never be stopped

Name: Restart-WinService.ps1
Description: Stops and restarts a named Windows service. Service name must be provided as an input.
Impact Notes: Service restart may briefly interrupt functionality provided by that service.

Name: Restart-WindowsUpdateServices.ps1
Description: Stops and restarts Windows Update and related services (wuauserv / BITS / cryptsvc / msiserver) to resolve stalled or broken update states. Supports a -ForceRestart switch.
Impact Notes: Services are temporarily unavailable during restart. No user-visible interruption unless an update was actively installing.

Name: Enable-HardwareAcceleratedGPUScheduling.ps1
Description: Enables Hardware-Accelerated GPU Scheduling (HAGS) via the registry. Reduces CPU overhead for GPU-heavy workloads. Requires Windows 10 2004+ and a WDDM 2.7+ GPU driver. A reboot is required for the change to take effect.
Impact Notes: Requires a reboot. May cause display instability on devices with drivers that do not fully support WDDM 2.7+.

Name: Disable-NonEssentialStartupApps.ps1
Description: Removes non-essential applications from the Windows startup sequence via Run registry keys and StartupApproved keys.
Impact Notes: User startup apps are disabled without notification. Users may be surprised that apps they configured no longer auto-launch at logon.
Additional Notes: Define which apps are considered non-essential before deploying at scale

Name: Invoke-AutoRemediateAudioBluetooth.ps1
Description: Runs sequenced audio and Bluetooth health checks and automatically remediates failures: restarts audio services (AudioSrv / AudioEndpointBuilder), refreshes errored PnP audio devices, corrects microphone access policy, and ensures the Bluetooth service is running.
Impact Notes: Audio service restarts cause a ~1-2 second interruption. Volume mute-state correction sends a keyboard keystroke. No dialogs or user prompts.

Name: Invoke-AutoRemediateDriverIssues.ps1
Description: Runs sequenced driver health checks across audio / Bluetooth / network adapter classes. Remediates via an escalating action chain controlled by -AllowedActions (Scan / Restart / Rollback / Reinstall / Uninstall) and scoped by -DriverFilter regex. Default actions are Scan + Restart + Rollback.
Impact Notes: Destructive actions (Reinstall / Uninstall) permanently remove devices from the PnP tree with no undo. A misconfigured -DriverFilter can remove network adapters leaving the device unreachable via MDM.
Additional Notes: Use default actions only unless a specific issue requires escalation. Test -DriverFilter patterns in a lab first. Never deploy Reinstall/Uninstall to NICs at scale.

Name: Invoke-AutoRemediateNetworkStack.ps1
Description: Automated network stack diagnostic and remediation. Checks and remediates network adapter state, TCP/IP stack health, DNS client service, and basic connectivity.
Impact Notes: Network stack reset operations briefly disrupt all network connectivity. DNS flush and adapter restart cause a short gap in network availability.

Name: Invoke-AutoRemediatePrinter.ps1
Description: Runs sequenced printer health checks (Print Spooler service / installed printers / offline state / default printer / queue health / driver status) and automatically remediates failures where possible.
Impact Notes: Print Spooler service restart cancels in-flight print jobs. Users must resubmit queued jobs after the remediation.

Name: Invoke-AutoRemediateWindowsUpdates.ps1
Description: Runs sequenced Windows Update health checks (WU / BITS / Cryptographic services / DataStore / pending reboot / policy). Automatically remediates service failures and optionally clears a corrupt DataStore.
Impact Notes: Services are briefly stopped and restarted. DataStore reset clears update history. Pending reboot is flagged but never forced.

Name: Invoke-SimpleReboot.ps1
Description: Displays a configurable countdown notification to the logged-on user then forces a reboot via shutdown.exe. Default countdown is 5 minutes.
Impact Notes: Forces a reboot after countdown. Unsaved work will be lost if the user does not act on the notification.

Name: Invoke_RebootWithUserDefer_1.2.0.7.ps1
Description: Advanced reboot orchestrator that notifies the user on a configurable schedule and forces a reboot after a maximum deferral window expires. Configurable countdown duration, notification interval, and maximum deferral hours.
Impact Notes: Reboot is ultimately forced after the max deferral window. Unsaved work will be lost if the user ignores all notifications.

Name: SmartReboot.ps1
Description: Detection component for the SmartReboot workflow. Checks the AirWatch SmartReboot registry key to determine if a pending install has completed. Returns exit 1641 (install pending) or 0 (complete).
Impact Notes: Detection only — does not initiate a reboot itself.
Additional Notes: Used as a detection script in WS1 to gate reboot-required logic

Name: Get-UserProfileSize.ps1
Description: Reports the size of all user profiles on the device. Useful for identifying disk space consumed by inactive or oversized profiles before running cleanup scripts.
Impact Notes: Read-only — no changes made to the device.

Name: Start-ClearRecycleBin.ps1
Description: Empties the Recycle Bin for all user profiles on the device silently.
Impact Notes: Permanently deletes Recycle Bin contents. Users cannot recover deleted files after this runs.

Name: Start-DiskCleanup.ps1
Description: Automates Windows Disk Cleanup (cleanmgr.exe) targeting system-level categories: previous Windows installations, update artifacts, error dumps, and upgrade logs. Reports GB reclaimed on completion.
Impact Notes: Deletes system cleanup categories which cannot be recovered. Previous Windows installation data is permanently removed.

Name: Start-UserProfileCleanup.ps1
Description: Removes inactive user profiles from the device based on configurable inactivity period (days) and size threshold (MB). Skips the configured enrollment user account.
Impact Notes: Deletes user profiles — all local data in those profiles is permanently lost. Misconfiguring the enrollment user exclusion can result in unintended profile deletion.
Additional Notes: Requires admin to configure ENROLLMENTUSER, DAYS_INACTIVE, and SIZE_THRESHOLD_MB before deploying

Name: Get-StartupApps.ps1
Description: Enumerates all startup entries for the currently logged-on user from 64-bit and 32-bit Run registry keys, startup folders, and Task Scheduler logon-trigger tasks. Reports name, enabled/disabled state, command, source, and user.
Impact Notes: Read-only — no changes made to the device.
Additional Notes: Runs as SYSTEM using SID-based registry access to target the logged-on user hive

Name: DEX_Invoke-SignoffWindows.ps1
Description: Signs off user sessions on a managed Windows device. Defaults to all sessions; use -CurrentUser switch to target only the active console session.
Impact Notes: Signs off user sessions — all unsaved work is lost. Ensure user notification is in place before deploying.

Name: DEX_LockDevice-BitlockEncryptionActivated.ps1
Description: Disables BitLocker protectors on C: then immediately initiates a hard shutdown. Intended for device decommission or emergency scenarios where encryption must be suspended before power-off.
Impact Notes: Forces an immediate shutdown — all unsaved work is lost. BitLocker protectors are disabled meaning the drive can be read without a recovery key until protectors are re-enabled.
Additional Notes: Use only for device decommission or specific security scenarios. Understand BitLocker implications before deploying.

Name: DEX_Start-LockDeviceSoft.ps1
Description: Soft-locks the active console session using tsdiscon.exe. Disconnects and locks the screen without signing the user out — all running processes and unsaved work are preserved.
Impact Notes: Screen is locked and session disconnected. User processes continue running in the background. No data loss.

Name: sensor-getservice.ps1
Description: Returns a JSON-compressed list of all currently running Windows services. Lightweight DEX sensor for service inventory and health monitoring.
Impact Notes: Read-only — no changes made to the device.

Name: Invoke-DefenderServiceRemediation.ps1
Description: Ensures core Microsoft Defender services (MDCoreSvc / WinDefend / WdNisSvc / mpssvc) are set to Automatic startup and running. Re-enables Real-Time Protection if it has been disabled.
Impact Notes: Service restarts may cause a brief gap in protection. Real-Time Protection toggling is instantaneous with no user-visible effect.

Name: Invoke-DefenderPauseScan.ps1
Description: Locates the highest available version of MpCmdRun.exe from the Defender platform directory and issues a scan cancel command. Safe to run when no scan is active.
Impact Notes: Cancels an active scan — scan progress is lost and must be re-triggered separately.

Name: Invoke-DefenderDynamicPerformance.ps1
Description: Calculates a hardware performance score from BIOS generation date, CPU core count, and system disk type (HDD/SSD/NVMe). Maps the score to a Defender CPU profile (VeryLow/Low/Medium/High) and applies matching ScanAvgCPULoadFactor and EnableLowCpuPriority settings via Set-MpPreference.
Impact Notes: Modifies Defender scan CPU thresholds only. No user-visible interruption.
Additional Notes: Useful for dynamically tuning Defender without assigning multiple MDM profiles per hardware tier

Name: Invoke-DefenderEnableCloudBlock_BAFS.ps1
Description: Enables Defender cloud protection settings: CloudBlockLevel (High/1), MAPSReporting (Advanced), and SubmitSamplesConsent (1 = send safe samples). All changes are idempotent and only applied if not already at the target value.
Impact Notes: No user-visible interruption. Increases cloud telemetry sent to Microsoft. Confirm SubmitSamplesConsent is appropriate for your data classification requirements.
Additional Notes: Confirm SubmitSamplesConsent is acceptable in regulated/restricted environments before deploying

Name: Invoke-DefenderUpdateSignatures.ps1
Description: Checks whether Defender virus definitions are out of date. If so triggers Update-MpSignature as a background job so the script exits within MDM timeout windows.
Impact Notes: Background signature update consumes network bandwidth. No user-visible interruption during script execution.

Name: Start-DefenderThreatReport_1.4.8.4.ps1
Description: Queries active Defender threats and maps detected threat names to the Windows Uninstall registry for reporting in Workspace ONE Intelligence. Threat records are persisted for a configurable number of hours.
Impact Notes: Registry write only — no remediation actions taken. Threat data written to the Uninstall key may appear as installed apps in some reporting views.
Additional Notes: Designed to run on a recurring trigger (every 4 hours) for near-real-time threat visibility in Intelligence

Name: defender_cloudblock_bafs.ps1
Description: Detection sensor: reports the current Defender Block-at-First-Sight (BAFS) and cloud protection state (CloudBlockLevel / MAPSReporting / SubmitSamplesConsent).
Impact Notes: Read-only — no changes made to the device.

Name: defender_computerstate_summary.ps1
Description: Detection sensor: reports a summary of Microsoft Defender computer protection state including RTP status, signature age, scan status, and service health.
Impact Notes: Read-only — no changes made to the device.

Name: defender_licensestatus.ps1
Description: Detection sensor: reports Microsoft Defender for Endpoint license and onboarding state via the OnboardingState registry key.
Impact Notes: Read-only — no changes made to the device.

Name: defender_signatureoutofdate.ps1
Description: Detection sensor: reports whether Microsoft Defender virus definition signatures are out of date via Get-MpComputerStatus.
Impact Notes: Read-only — no changes made to the device.

Name: Invoke-CrowdStrikeServiceRemediation.ps1
Description: Ensures CrowdStrike Falcon services are running and set to Automatic startup. Note: Falcon is tamper-protected and cloud-managed — service remediation may be blocked by design. Confirm with your security team before deploying.
Impact Notes: CrowdStrike service restart may cause a brief gap in endpoint protection. Falcon tamper protection may actively block service state changes.
Additional Notes: Verify with security team that service remediation is permitted in your Falcon policy before deploying

Name: Invoke-TrellixCPUPriority.ps1
Description: Sets Trellix (formerly McAfee) endpoint security processes to BelowNormal CPU priority to reduce performance impact during active scans. Priority change is in-process only and does not persist across service restarts.
Impact Notes: CPU priority change is transparent to the user. No service interruption.
Additional Notes: Lab-tested only — validate in production before broad deployment

Name: Invoke-TrellixLocalUpdate.ps1
Description: Forces a local content update for Trellix Endpoint Security (amcfg.exe /update) then triggers a policy refresh via CmdAgent.exe. Supports both McAfee and Trellix installation paths.
Impact Notes: Update and policy refresh run in the background. No user-visible interruption.
Additional Notes: Lab-tested only — validate in production before broad deployment

Name: Invoke-TrellixRefreshPolicies.ps1
Description: Forces a policy refresh for Trellix (formerly McAfee) Endpoint Security via CmdAgent.exe steps: collect/send properties, send events, and check for new policies.
Impact Notes: Policy refresh runs in the background. No user-visible interruption.
Additional Notes: Lab-tested only — validate in production before broad deployment

Name: Invoke-TrendMicroServiceRemediation.ps1
Description: Ensures Trend Micro Apex One / OfficeScan services (ntrtscan / tmlisten / ofcservice / TmCCSF / TmWSCSvc / TmPfw) are running and set to Automatic startup.
Impact Notes: Trend Micro service restart may cause a brief gap in endpoint protection.
Additional Notes: Lab-tested only — validate in production before broad deployment

Name: performance_bitlockerstatus.ps1
Description: Detection sensor: reports the BitLocker protection status and encryption state of the C: drive.
Impact Notes: Read-only — no changes made to the device.

Name: performance_defender_scanorupdateactive.ps1
Description: Detection sensor: reports whether a Microsoft Defender scan or signature update is currently active. Useful for correlating Defender activity with high-CPU events in DEX.
Impact Notes: Read-only — no changes made to the device.

Name: performance_windowsupdate_status.ps1
Description: Detection sensor: reports the current Windows Update activity status (Idle / Downloading / Installing / etc.).
Impact Notes: Read-only — no changes made to the device.

Name: Measure-GPLoadTime.ps1
Description: Records Group Policy Client Side Extension (CSE) load times for the logged-on user to the registry. Mines the Microsoft-Windows-GroupPolicy event log for the most recent GP cycle and captures total duration and top 5 slowest CSEs.
Impact Notes: Read and registry write only. No user-visible interruption. Designed to run at logon via a SYSTEM scheduled task.

Name: Measure-GPLoadTimeEx.ps1
Description: Extended version of Measure-GPLoadTime with additional metric collection and GPO correlation detail.
Impact Notes: Read and registry write only. No user-visible interruption.

Name: Measure-LogonDuration.ps1
Description: Records key Windows logon duration metrics for the logged-on user to the registry by mining event logs. Captures credential provider, Group Policy, logon script, and shell load phases. Supports scheduled task deployment mode for recurring logon measurement.
Impact Notes: Read and registry write only. Designed to run at logon as a SYSTEM scheduled task. No user-visible interruption.

Name: Measure-LogonDurationEx.ps1
Description: Extended version of Measure-LogonDuration with additional logon phase coverage and metric detail.
Impact Notes: Read and registry write only. No user-visible interruption.

Name: Install-TroubleshootWizard.ps1
Description: Installs the TroubleshootWizard self-service troubleshooting tool to C:\ProgramData\AirWatch\Extensions\TroubleshootWizard. Supports SFD (Software Distribution) and ZIP (Product Provisioning) deployment modes with auto-detection.
Impact Notes: Copies files to ProgramData. No user-visible interruption.

Name: Uninstall-TroubleshootWizard.ps1
Description: Removes the TroubleshootWizard installation from C:\ProgramData\AirWatch\Extensions\TroubleshootWizard.
Impact Notes: Removes the self-service troubleshooting tool — users will no longer be able to launch it.

Name: TroubleshootWizard.ps1
Description: Core TroubleshootWizard engine. Orchestrates the self-service troubleshooting experience by loading diagnostic steps, presenting a UI to the user, and executing selected remediations.
Impact Notes: Runs diagnostic and remediation steps selected by the user. Impact depends on which troubleshooting steps are executed.

Name: Create-ZipPackage.ps1
Description: Packages TroubleshootWizard payload files into a ZIP archive for Product Provisioning deployments where only a single file can be staged.
Impact Notes: Admin/build utility — not deployed to endpoints. No runtime impact.
